**Fukui reactivity analysis (condensed Fukui functions f+, f-, dual descriptor)** — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, exact four-center integrals, no density fitting) in PySCF (via chemkit), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: read from `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz`; this was a single-point DFT calculation on the input geometry (no DFT-level optimization). SCF converged; integrity status ok, trustworthy. Warnings from integrity checks (verbatim):
- sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
- sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
- common header present

The analysis used Mulliken partial charges to compute f+ (nucleophilic attack) and f- (electrophilic attack) for each of the 8 atoms. The most electrophilic site is atom 4 (the β-carbon) with f+ = 0.64232102, and the most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (oxygen) with f- = 0.39388616. Output JSON written to `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp7y1ypqmz/live.json`; PNG plot at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/argo_o4-mini/20260704-174515_acrolein_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`.
